title: jasminum paucinervium
intro: Jasminum paucinervium, also known as the few-veined jasmine or few-nerved jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, native to India and Sri Lanka. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Jasminum paucinervium include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Jasminum paucinervium is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
